hypothesis: If a CNN model is first pre-trained on a data-rich, additive-free SEM image dataset of NCM cathodes and subsequently fine-tuned on a smaller target dataset containing additive-induced samples, then it will achieve higher nine-class (three compositions × three aging states) classification accuracy and F1 scores on the target dataset than a model trained from scratch on the target data, because transfer learning bridges data-rich and data-poor SEM image domains.
